title: Hellbringer - New Expansion - Diablo-Inspired Card Game
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6Xmd08nL2G4

---

Daniel (@dungeondive) dives into the new Bestiary and Forbidden Spells expansion for Hellbringer, a Diablo-themed card game focused on character building and engine generation rather than dungeon crawling. This expansion adds substantial depth to an already engaging system.

Hellbringer is fundamentally about building your character and defeating bosses through acquiring loot and upgrading stats. You manage health as defense, unlock perks, and socket gems into weapons. The base game came with two essential expansions (Quests & Events, and Merchant & Loot) that Daniel argues should be included by default—they're that important to the experience.

The new expansion introduces two fresh classes (the Alchemist and Necromancer), over 40 new enemies, elite monsters, and powerful dark energy dice that scale up to 15 damage points. New features include expanded loot decks, additional starting weapons for existing classes, a solo mode mule with improved mechanics, and upgraded cards throughout the system. The upgrade pack includes minor but meaningful balance changes and clarifications to existing cards and mechanisms.

Daniel appreciates the expansion's focus on variety without overwhelming complexity. The new loot keeps the character-building loop exciting, and the elite monsters add late-game challenge. His main suggestion: expand the event deck further to match the increased variety everywhere else. Overall, Hellbringer continues to deliver that satisfying power fantasy of building an increasingly broken character engine—perfect for solo card game enthusiasts.
